# Kaspa Forge

> Three non-custodial services on the Kaspa L1 blockchain, sharing one origin and one encrypted in-browser profile: Kaspa Safe (a vault where theft can be cancelled), Kaspa Escrow (an escrow whose guarantor physically can't steal), and Kaspa Marketplace (listings with escrow built in). No accounts, no custody — funds sit in on-chain covenant contracts that can only pay predefined parties. Built and operated by the OfficeForge AI office.

Key facts:

- Keys are generated and stay in the user's browser (the "Desk" profile, an encrypted key file); the service never sees or stores private keys.
- Kaspa Safe: a personal KAS vault on a covenant — every withdrawal waits out a delay the owner sets, and a separate alarm key can cancel a theft in progress; optional inheritance path for heirs.
- The vault's alarm key lives on a separate "alarm card" (shown once at creation, deliberately not stored in the key file and not derivable from it): stealing the key file and its password is never enough to empty a vault.
- The covenant's migrate path (hot + alarm signatures together = full owner authority) moves a whole vault instantly with a free destination — key rotation, contract-version upgrades, or an instant escape after cancelling a theft; available via the open-source vaultctl CLI.
- Kaspa Escrow: P2P deals of 50–10,000 KAS (open beta). Creating a deal is free; the fee is paid on the outcome — 0.5% (min 1.2 KAS) on release/refund/mutual settlement, 2% (min 5 KAS) on arbitration; the emergency on-chain timeout path has no service fee and means a deal can never hang, even if the website disappears.
- Dispute resolution is a rail, not a favor: an AI mediator reads the deal and proposes a non-binding outcome in minutes; a human arbiter has the final word; auto-release protects the seller from silent buyers. The covenant caps where funds can go no matter who decides: buyer, seller, or the visible fee.
- The deal chat is end-to-end encrypted on-chain messaging on the Kaspa BlockDAG (Kasia protocol), photos and videos included — it cannot be forged or backdated, and becomes the case file in a dispute.
- Kaspa Marketplace: physical goods, OTC trades and services priced in KAS. The Buy button doesn't pay the seller — it opens an escrow deal with the listing's terms. Listings are free, AI-moderated (text and photos), stay live for 30 days.
- Open source: contract, arbiter tool and offline recovery are public.

## Products

- [Kaspa Forge — overview](https://kaspaforge.org/): umbrella page for all three services and how they share one Desk profile
- [Kaspa Safe](https://kaspaforge.org/safe.html): how the covenant vault works — withdrawal delay, alarm key, inheritance
- [Kaspa Escrow](https://kaspaforge.org/escrow-index.html): deal mechanics in three steps, the dispute rail, fees, and FAQ
- [Kaspa Marketplace](https://kaspaforge.org/market.html): escrow-first listings — how buying and selling works, costs, FAQ
- [Forge Contracts (deal templates)](https://kaspaforge.org/contracts.html): a catalog of ready-made escrow deal templates — hire a developer, buy goods, OTC swap, rental deposit and more — each preset with sensible deadlines, a recommended deposit and an evidence checklist, deep-linking into `escrow.html?template=<id>`

## Documentation (how it works & security)

- [Docs overview](https://kaspaforge.org/docs/): the covenant idea, the four pieces, the security model in five verifiable claims, honest beta limits
- [The Desk & the master key file](https://kaspaforge.org/docs/desk.html): the age-encrypted in-browser profile, master-seed derivation (one backup covers future vaults/deals — except alarm keys, which live on alarm cards by design), restore on any device, offline decryptor, what the server can and cannot see
- [How Kaspa Safe works](https://kaspaforge.org/docs/safe.html): the vault covenant's seven spend paths (initiate/cancel/complete/check-in/inherit×2/migrate), the three keys and the alarm card, funding mechanics, inheritance modes, costs, full security model
- [How Kaspa Escrow works](https://kaspaforge.org/docs/escrow.html): the escrow covenant's spend paths and why no path pays the arbiter, deal lifecycle, dispute windows, fees, the Kasia encrypted chat, AI mediator + human arbiter, timeout guarantees
- [How the Kaspa Marketplace works](https://kaspaforge.org/docs/market.html): Buy button → escrow deal, listing types and moderation, shipping geography, seller mechanics, security model
- Russian versions: [/ru/docs/](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/docs/), [desk](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/docs/desk.html), [safe](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/docs/safe.html), [escrow](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/docs/escrow.html), [market](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/docs/market.html)

## Guides & policies

- [Recover a Safe offline](https://kaspaforge.org/recover.html): reopening a vault on any device without the website
- [Recover an escrow deal offline](https://kaspaforge.org/recover-escrow.html): the deal outlives the website — recovery sheet and on-chain timeout
- [Privacy — Kaspa Safe](https://kaspaforge.org/privacy.html)
- [Privacy — Escrow & Marketplace](https://kaspaforge.org/privacy-escrow.html)
- [Source code on GitHub](https://github.com/Kaspaforge/kaspaforge): covenant contracts, WASM core, arbiter tooling

- [Kaspa Safe (RU)](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/safe.html)
- [Kaspa Escrow — Russian version (RU)](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/escrow-index.html)
- [Kaspa Marketplace — Russian version (RU)](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/market.html)
- [Forge Contracts — Russian version (RU)](https://kaspaforge.org/ru/contracts.html)

## Company

- [OfficeForge](https://officeforge.co): the self-hosted AI office that built and runs Kaspa Forge ("Run on OfficeForge")
- Contact: kaspa@officeforge.co
